framework_source: UK Government Digital, Data and Technology (DDaT) Capability Framework

use_case_per_ir8477_section3.target_audience: WIDAI framework developers, international workforce planners, and stakeholders evaluating WIDAI's applicability beyond US-centric frameworks. Secondary audience: organizations building data/AI workforce competency programs with UK government alignment requirements.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.why_this_mapping: DDaT is the UK government's capability framework for digital, data, and technology professionals. It is the first international (non-US) framework in the STRM sequence, providing geographic diversification after three US-origin frameworks (O*NET, NICE, DCWF). DDaT defines skill levels across job families including Data, Technology, and IT Operations — domains with direct WIDAI overlap. This STRM validates WIDAI coverage against a framework that uses fundamentally different vocabulary and structural conventions than US frameworks.

concept_types: Skills and competencies across DDaT job families — 189 elements
focal_document.exclusion_rationale: No elements excluded. All 189 DDaT skill/competency elements evaluated. Elements from job families outside WIDAI scope (e.g., purely digital delivery roles) produce lower-strength matches that document the boundary.

concept_types_mapped.structural_note: DDaT uses a job family / role / skill structure rather than the KSAT building-block model used by NICE and DCWF. DDaT skills are described as capability statements at varying levels of abstraction. This structural difference means the FDE granularity varies — some DDaT elements are comparable to individual WIDAI KSAs, others span multiple competency areas.

use_case_per_ir8477_section3.direction_of_mapping: Focal → Reference. Each DDaT skill/competency element (focal) is evaluated against the WIDAI KSA pool (reference). A DDaT element with 'No relationship' to any WIDAI KSA indicates either (a) a UK-specific digital delivery competency outside WIDAI scope, or (b) a potential gap in WIDAI coverage.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.exhaustiveness_level: Exhaustive. Every DDaT skill/competency element (189) is evaluated. For each element, the most semantically relevant WIDAI KSA is identified via bi-encoder candidate identification, then scored through the full multi-method pipeline (identical to STRM-001 through STRM-003).
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.rationale_type: Semantic (primary). DDaT and WIDAI use different vocabularies — DDaT uses UK government digital/data terminology while WIDAI uses US-influenced data/AI terminology. Semantic rationale evaluates whether differently-worded concepts describe overlapping competencies despite the transatlantic vocabulary gap.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.rationale_justification: DDaT is a UK government capability framework. WIDAI is a data and AI workforce framework. The domain overlap exists in data management, data architecture, technology operations, and governance — areas where both frameworks define professional competencies regardless of national context. Semantic rationale captures the conceptual overlap beneath the vocabulary differences.

scope_decisions.note: All 189 DDaT elements evaluated without categorical exclusions. Zero 'No relationship' results — every DDaT element maps to at least one WIDAI KSA, reflecting the tight scope overlap between DDaT's data/technology job families and WIDAI's data/AI domain.

structural_elements_excluded.job_families: Job family categories are organizational groupings, not FDEs.
structural_elements_excluded.role_levels: Skill level descriptors (awareness, working, practitioner, expert) are maturity indicators, not competency statements.

candidate_identification.description: Bi-encoder embeddings (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) used to identify the most semantically relevant WIDAI KSA candidate for each DDaT element. Preprocessing step for pairing, not a scoring method.
candidate_identification.note: Candidate identification determines which WIDAI KSA each DDaT element is paired with. All scoring, classification, and significance generation performed by strm/ddat/strm_scoring_pipeline.py, methodologically identical to STRM-001 through STRM-003.
